label: 150_mexican_lpez_lpez obrador_obrador
description: The deteriorating U.S.-Mexico security relationship due to Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador's policies and the potential impact of new laws restricting foreign law enforcement. The challenges of criminal violence in Mexico amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

earliest_articles.title: Mexico: A Bad Case of the Blues
earliest_articles.excerpt: A recent visit to Mexico City found Mexicans despondent. Mexicans from distinct backgrounds and with differing political perspectives moaned. Unemployment at the historic rate of 6.2 percent, a 9 percent fall in the economic activity index, dwindling oil resources and a Standard & Poor’s downgrading of Mexican…

earliest_articles.title: México: Un grave caso de depresión
earliest_articles.excerpt: Durante una visita reciente a la Ciudad de México, los mexicanos se mostraron abatidos. Habitantes de distintos orígenes y con diferentes perspectivas políticas se lamentaban. earliest_articles.title: How to Win Mexico’s Drug War
earliest_articles.excerpt: On Tuesday this week, a high-powered drug war summit convened in Mexico City with Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Defense Secretary Robert Gates, and Department of Homeland Security Secretary Janet Napolitano in attendance. newest_articles.title: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is cleaning house and consolidating power
newest_articles.excerpt: On February 3, Mexican authorities arrested the mayor of Tequila, Jalisco, along with three other local officials, on charges of extorting beer and tequila companies and collaborating with the powerful Cartel Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG). newest_articles.title: How could the Mexican government respond to US military actions?
newest_articles.excerpt: In the past week, the Trump administration has significantly intensified pressure on the Mexican government to allow the U.S. military to take direct actions against drug cartels inside Mexico. Going beyond U.S. air strikes against drug labs and cartel bosses, the U.S. demands appear to include U.S. soldiers on the…

central_articles.title: The US-Mexico security relationship in 2021
central_articles.excerpt: Vanda Felbab-Brown outlines what she calls the steady deterioration of the U.S.-Mexico bilateral security relationship and the mismanagement of and apathy toward public safety in Mexico by Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador; and what the Biden administration can do. This piece is part of a series titled…

central_articles.title: A dangerous backtrack on the US-Mexico security relationship
central_articles.excerpt: A new law passed by the Mexican Congress restricting the operations of foreign law enforcement agents in Mexico would put the U.S.-Mexico security relationship back into the deep freezer of the early 1990s if implemented, writes Vanda Felbab-Brown. This piece was originally published by Mexico Today under the title,…

central_articles.title: Crime and anti-crime security policy in Mexico in 2020
central_articles.excerpt: COVID-19’s toll on Mexico should not obscure its challenges in respect to criminal violence and failing anti-crime efforts, writes Vanda Felbab-Brown.
